verses.commentary: What causes fights and quarrels? Desires (hēdonai, ἡδοναί: pleasures, lusts) that battle within. Conflict is external expression of internal war. Sin in the heart produces strife in relationships. The problem is not circumstances but the sinful self.

You desire but do not have, so you kill. You covet but cannot get, so you quarrel. Unfulfilled desires lead to violence and conflict. Murder may be literal or metaphorical (hatred, slander). You do not have because you do not ask God. Prayerlessness is rooted in self-reliance.
You ask but do not receive because you ask with wrong motives. Prayer for selfish pleasure is not answered. God does not serve our lusts. Right prayer aligns with God's will, not our appetites.

You adulterous people. Spiritual adultery: covenant unfaithfulness. Friendship with the world is enmity against God. The world system opposed to God. Choosing the world makes you an enemy of God. Allegiance is binary: God or world, not both.

God jealously longs for the spirit He caused to dwell in us. Difficult verse. God is jealous for His people. He does not tolerate divided loyalty. Jealousy here is righteous: God's passionate desire for undivided love.

But He gives more grace. God's grace is greater than our sin. Proverbs 3:34 quoted: God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble. Pride resists grace; humility receives it. Grace flows to the lowly.

Submit to God. Resist the devil. Two commands. Submission to God enables resistance to Satan. The devil will flee from you. Victory over temptation requires allegiance to God. Submission is not passivity; it is positioning under divine authority.

Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. Relational reciprocity. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded. External and internal cleansing. Hands represent actions; hearts represent motives. Both must be purified.

Grieve, mourn, wail. Change laughter to mourning, joy to gloom. Call to repentance. Sorrow over sin precedes restoration. Flippancy about sin is incompatible with holiness. Godly grief leads to life (2 Corinthians 7:10).

Humble yourselves before the Lord, and He will lift you up. Exaltation comes through humility. God exalts the humble and humbles the exalted. The way up is down. Pride brings judgment; humility brings grace.

Do not slander one another. Slander (katalaleo, καταλαλέω): speak against, defame. Speaking against a brother judges the law and positions oneself above it. Slander usurps God's authority. To judge the law is to reject it.

There is only one Lawgiver and Judge: God, who can save and destroy. Who are you to judge your neighbor? Human judgment is arrogance. God alone has authority to judge. We are accountable to Him, not lords over others.

You who say today or tomorrow we will go to this city, do business, and make money. Presumption about the future. Planning without acknowledging God's sovereignty. Life is uncertain; plans are fragile.

You do not know what will happen tomorrow. What is your life? A mist that appears and vanishes. Life is brief, fragile, uncertain. Human existence is vapor. James echoes Psalm 39:5, Job 7:7. Mortality confronts presumption.

Instead, say if it is the Lord's will, we will live and do this or that. Submission to divine providence. All plans are contingent on God's will. Acknowledging God's sovereignty is wisdom. The phrase if the Lord wills recognizes human dependence.

As it is, you boast in your arrogant schemes. All such boasting is evil. Arrogance (alazoneia, ἀλαζονεία): presumptuous pride. Boasting in self-sufficiency is sin. Humility acknowledges dependence; pride asserts autonomy.

If anyone knows the good they ought to do and does not do it, it is sin. Sin of omission, not just commission. Knowing and not doing is culpable. Responsibility increases with knowledge. James anticipates chapter 5: failure to act justly is sin.
